Choice Specs.description: Mega Alakazam is a powerful special wallbreaker and revenge killer by virtue of its nearly unparalleled Speed tier and sky-high Special Attack. Moongeist Beam provides coverage against opposing Psychic-types and Imposter users, and it also bypasses Ice Scales. Fleur Cannon OHKOes Yveltal and Dragon-types such as Mega Garchomp and Miraidon. Armor Cannon is the best coverage option against Steel-types like Mega Steelix and Registeel. Beads of Ruin provides a general power boost to all moves, but Psychic Surge allows Mega Alakazam to use Expanding Force, which is more spammable than Psycho Boost, and Psystrike, which crushes Blissey and RegenVest users like Primal Kyogre. Mega Alakazam appreciates support from Imposter users and Knock Off users to remove Assault Vest from RegenVest Pokemon that could otherwise wall what move it locks into and Leftovers from Blissey to make it easier to wear down. Entry hazard support from RegenVest users with Stone Axe and Spikes users such as Eternatus can help Mega Alakazam achieve 2HKOes on targets like Ice Scales Arceus formes. RegenVest users like Primal Kyogre can additionally provide slow pivoting to bring Mega Alakazam in safely, as it is frail and susceptible to status conditions; these can also Imposter-proof Mega Alakazam if it isn't using Psystrike. Other Imposter-proofing options include Imposter Chansey and Ice Scales walls neutral to its attacks like Ho-Oh.

Physical Wall.description: Mega Slowbro's insanely high Defense and good typing make it an incredibly hard to break Fur Coat wall on balance teams, checking and forcing out common metagame threats like Mega Garchomp and Kyurem-B. Scald threatens to burn setup threats like Ice Scales Arceus-Ground, which could otherwise set up in Mega Slowbro's face, and it also deals solid damage. Future Sight has incredible synergy with Teleport, as it allows Mega Slowbro to support its offensive teammates by preventing opposing walls from safely switching in. Alternatively, Mortal Spin allows Mega Slowbro to serve as an entry hazard remover and cripple foes unaffected by burn, such as Ho-Oh. Covert Cloak prevents Mega Slowbro from being chipped by foes reliant on Salt Cure and Mortal Spin to make progress, while Rocky Helmet consistently chips wallbreakers in exchange for a worse matchup against passive walls like Mega Audino; Rocky Helmet also notably punishes Choice Band foes, such as Kyurem-B, for trying to easily up chip damage with U-turn. The pivoting and Future Sight support Mega Slowbro provides is heavily appreciated by wallbreakers that often fall just shy of 2HKOs, such as Mega Garchomp and Ash-Greninja; teammates with a bad matchup against various physical attackers are also appreciative of its walling capabilities, including the likes of Ice Scales Ho-Oh and Blissey. The aforementioned Blissey and other special walls like Ice Scales Arceus-Ground and Ho-Oh can also cover for dangerous foes like Miraidon and Flutter Mane, forming a solid defensive core. Since Ash-Greninja is one of the only wallbreakers that can dissuade Mega Slowbro from switching in, a check to it like Miraidon or Fur Coat Arceus-Fairy is appreciated. Mega Slowbro should be Imposter-proofed by a Take Heart user, ideally with Thunder Cage, as even if Mega Slowbro is able to burn or poison the Imposter user, it still acts as a free source of healing and Teleport momentum.

Chansey.overview:
Imposter Chansey is the single most defining set in BH. With astronomical HP that is preserved after transforming compounded with a potential Eviolite boost, it can turn into a significantly bulkier version of the foe, making it a blanket check to most offensive Pokemon. Defensive Pokemon are not safe either, as without proper teambuilding, Imposter users can take advantage of their enormous bulk to become incredibly difficult to punish, allowing them to utilize the constant PP Imposter can provide to stall out the foes. As a result of these traits, every team must Imposter-proof: be well prepared for and carry responses to all of its own Pokemon. Imposter also provides invaluable scouting information in a tier where anything is possible to allow the player to make more informed decisions. Additionally, Imposter Chansey and Blissey provide role compression by Imposter-proofing some special attackers when not transformed. However, every competent team will prepare carefully for Imposter, limiting its effectiveness; examples of common generally Imposter-resilient sets include Plate + Judgment users like Arceus-Ghost and Thunder Cage + Strength Sap users like Arceus-Electric. Imposter users also dislike the metagame having an abundance of offensive Pokemon such as Eternatus and Mega Garchomp that are weak to their own STAB moves, reducing their defensive utility and making them more difficult to fit on teams.

Alternatively, non-Imposter Chansey utilizes its astronomical special bulk and respectable physical bulk with Eviolite to serve as a strong special wall capable of answering threats like Eternatus and Take Heart Arceus formes. It differentiates itself from Blissey through the extra bulk from said item allowing it to sponge certain physical coverage moves like Eternatus's Shell Side Arm much more comfortably. Its exceptional special bulk also lets it forgo Ice Scales in favor of Magic Guard, allowing it to be very resilient against residual damage from entry hazards, Thunder Cage, and status conditions. Chansey's minuscule Attack also lets it neutralize Strength Sap healing from the special attackers it aims to wall to support its offensive teammates. However, Chansey is rather passive, causing it to be a momentum drain against Pokemon such as RegenVest users with Knock Off and Flutter Mane, which an issue amplified by its lack of passive recovery from Leftovers, forcing it to spend turns healing more. It also has a minor opportunity cost of not being able to use Eviolite + Imposter.
Chansey.comments:
Other Options

Imposter users can run a variety of other utility moves depending on what the team requires, with some examples being Court Change, Rapid Spin, and Glare. They can also use some other niche items. Leftovers provides a minor longevity increase and crucially gives them an edge against otherwise troublesome Take Heart Arceus formes. Spooky Plate lets them surprise Normalize Flutter Mane and non-Knock Off Arceus-Ghost, although these are its exclusive use cases. Choice Scarf is very niche, and the Choice lock can be highly restrictive and exploitable, with the only benefit being safer matchups against certain offensive Pokemon, mainly Dragon-types like Ultra Necrozma and Mega Garchomp. Non-Imposter Chansey can utilize a Regenerator set to spend less turns healing but faces fierce competition from Blissey due to a vulnerability to entry hazards, with the extra security against Shell Side Arm Eternatus being offset by potential poison.

Checks and Counters

Knock Off: Imposter users hate having their items removed, whether it's the significant bulk cut from losing Eviolite or losing the high utility Heavy-Duty Boots and Covert Cloak provide. Non-Imposter Chansey is heavily reliant on Eviolite for its bulk, making Knock Off incredibly crippling.

Status Conditions: Paralysis cripples Imposter users' ability to check notable offensive threats and allows common Dragon Energy users like Eternatus to comfortably defeat them. Poison can also rapidly chip them down.

Anti-Imposter Sets: Due to Imposter's prevalence, many common sets are capable of defeating Imposter users one-on-one. Notable examples include Thunder Cage + Strength Sap Arceus formes and Judgment + Plate users like Arceus-Ghost, Arceus-Steel, and Flutter Mane.

Wallbreakers with Super Effective Coverage: Powerful wallbreakers such as Eternatus, Ultra Necrozma, and Mega Garchomp can hit Imposter users extremely hard, deterring them from switching in. Dragon Energy users in particular can threaten weakened Imposter users significantly. These will often need to consider Speed ties against Imposter users, however, so may still switch out.

Limiting Healing: Imposter users can be susceptible to being worn down rapidly if they is unable to transform into something that can reliably heal. This most commonly involves Strength Sap and limiting its healing through low Attack Pokemon like Chansey, Blissey, and Mega Audino.

Trapping: Imposter only grants a total of 20 PP, so sets with a trapping move like Spirit Shackle, Block, or the rare Jaw Lock can trap and PP stall Imposter users. However, Spirit Shackle users must be cautious of being reverse PP stalled by Covert Cloak Imposter users in extended games.

Physical Wallbreakers: Despite the Eviolite boost, Chansey is still rather frail physically and is vulnerable to physical wallbreakers like Mega Garchomp and Mega Blaziken, though Burning Bulwark can somewhat alleviate the issue against Choice Band users.

Flutter Mane: Flutter Mane regularly runs Pain Split and Spikes, allowing to make significant progress against Chansey while keeping itself healthy.

Taunt: Chansey is highly vulnerable to Taunt due to its passivity. Taunt is capable of shutting down its recovery, can stop its setup control, and often prevents its pivoting attempts.

Imposter.description:
Most of the time, Chansey will be transformed into the foe, so the given moves are for the situations it is not transformed. Shore Up in conjunction with Topsy-Turvy or Haze allows Chansey to excellently Imposter-proof special attackers such as Miraidon and Take Heart Arceus formes with its high special bulk and low Attack to limit Strength Sap healing from opposing Imposter users. Block can be used to ensure that opposing Imposter users that stay in are trapped and eliminated, while Teleport provides slow pivoting instead. Heal Bell lets Chansey act as a cleric in a pinch, while Jungle Healing can provide additional recovery while also curing it of status, which is useful to Imposter-proof Eternatus. Wish is an alternative if Teleport is run to effectively support teammates. Eviolite provides a generally useful bulk increase that is particularly helpful against threats like Mega Alakazam. Heavy-Duty Boots is the premier option on entry hazard-resilient teams that forgo hazard removal. Covert Cloak meanwhile blocks the secondary effect of moves like Spirit Shackle and Mortal Spin, allowing it to bypass some Imposter-proofing methods. It is worth noting that if Eviolite is not opted for, then Blissey can be used as the Imposter user.

Imposter users can fit on virtually every team archetype due to the utility and information they provide, and all Pokemon can benefit from their presence. Physically offensive Pokemon such as Choice Band Ash-Greninja and Mega Garchomp appreciate the scouting information Imposter brings, in particular moves like Burning Bulwark being revealed; these Pokemon are also particularly helpful for Teleport sets, providing an immediate method to force out opposing Imposter users transformed into Chansey. Special attackers like Eternatus and Mega Gardevoir meanwhile enjoy Imposter Chansey's ability to trade Knock Offs against opposing RegenVest Pokemon to soften them up, and these attackers can also be potentially Imposter-proofed by it. Defensively, walls appreciate Imposter Chansey alleviating pressure by soft checking threats like Mega Alakazam. Pokemon that provide slow pivoting, such as Mega Audino, can bring Imposter users in to safely scout opponents. Special walls like Ice Scales Mega Audino can check a variety of threats to Imposter users, such as Arceus-Ghost, Take Heart Arceus formes, and offensive Dragon-types like Mega Sceptile. Imposter users can also be highly reliant on their items, especially in the case of Heavy-Duty Boots and Covert Cloak, so they appreciates Knock Off absorbers like Magic Guard Arceus Ghost and select RegenVest users.

Blissey.overview: Blissey is a solid special wall thanks to its gargantuan HP and good Special Defense, allowing it to tank almost any special attack. This special bulk allows Blissey to forgo Ice Scales for abilities like Magic Guard and Regenerator that give it extra survivability against all forms of chip damage. Its low Attack also lets it deny Strength Sap's healing from most special attackers like Miraidon and Arceus-Fairy. Blissey has the fatal flaw of having nonexistent Defense, so it is forced out by physical attackers; however, this can also be used to your advantage to offensively Imposter-proof Blissey with said attackers. Blissey faces competition from Chansey as a Magic Guard user because of Chansey's increased bulk from Eviolite; however, Blissey has the advantage of not being deterred as much by Knock Off. Additionally, Blissey is quite passive, especially against Regenerator users, so it can struggle to progress the game apart from slow pivoting.
Blissey.comments:
Other Options

Status-inducing moves such as Glare and Toxic are potential options on the Magic Guard set, but they make Blissey far too passive without providing other utility to be worth it. Miscellaneous utility abilities like Drought and Pressure can be utilized on specific teams, but they are generally niche.

Checks and Counters

Physical Attackers: Blissey's abysmal Defense means that virtually any physical attacker threatens it. They have to be careful of Burning Bulwark, however.

Attackers That Target Defense: In a similar vein, Blissey cannot check mixed attackers like High Jump Kick Mega Sceptile and Secret Sword or Psystrike users like Arceus-Ghost and Mega Gardevoir, since they target Blissey's Defense stat.

Regenerator Users: Regenerator users like Ting-Lu can shrug off Ruinations from Blissey and use it as an entry point for their various utility.

Pain Split Flutter Mane: With Pain Split, Flutter Mane can heavily damage Blissey while healing itself to full HP every time, wasting Blissey's recovery PP and weakening it so other foes such as Eternatus can break through it.

Magic Guard.description:
With Magic Guard, Blissey is immune to all kinds of passive damage like entry hazards, Thunder Cage chip damage, and poison. This lets Blissey very consistently check most special attackers without fear of said passive damage cutting into its longevity. Shore Up is the preferred move option thanks to its consistency, though Wish can also be used as an alternative for more PP and the ability to heal other teammates. Ruination, compared with the Strength Sap blocking potential of Blissey, will completely brick most Strength Sap reliant foes that aren't physical while efficiently wasting recovery PP from other foes that rely on it. It also prevents Blissey from being entirely passive, though it's still questionable against Regenerator users. Topsy-Turvy deters setup from various special attackers, like Torch Song Mega Sceptile and Take Heart Arceus-Fairy. Burning Bulwark scouts moves, especially from Choice Band users like Mega Garchomp thinking they can use Blissey as a free entry point to start wallbreaking only to get permanently crippled by a burn, while additionally letting Blissey use Wish safely. However, it can make Blissey hard to Imposter-proof offensively with your own physical attackers. Teleport is the preferred pivoting move thanks to its negative priority, but Parting Shot can also be used to annoy switch-ins with lowered offenses, and U-turn bypasses Taunt.

Blissey's ability to block Strength Sap's healing means that virtually every single Choice Band user appreciates it, especially ones that are slower and thus more vulnerable to Strength Sap neutralizing them like Mega Blaziken. Additionally, they can also reverse Imposter-proof Blissey if it's not carrying Burning Bulwark. Other teammates like RegenVest Celesteela and Ice Scales Arceus-Steel appreciate Blissey checking threats they cannot, such as Encore Miraidon, alongside the occasional Imposter-proofing that Blissey can provide. Blissey is vulnerable to foes that run coverage specifically for it like Shell Side Arm Eternatus and Secret Sword Arceus-Ghost, strong physical attackers like Ash-Greninja using it as an entry point, and mixed attackers like Ultra Necrozma. Therefore solid checks to them like Fur Coat Mega Audino, Ice Scales Arceus-Ground, and RegenVest Ting-Lu make for good partners. Fast offensive teammates like Koraidon also synergize well with Wish variants of Blissey to revenge kill setup threats weakened by Ruination.

Regenerator.description: Blissey uses its enormous special bulk and minimal Attack to simultaneously wall special threats and disrupt their Strength Sap recovery while maintaining momentum from Regenerator. Knock Off is a staple move on RegenVest users, while Wish in conjunction with Blissey's HP helps keep teammates healthy. Ruination reduces Blissey's passivity, though Salt Cure is an alternative that can be more effective into Pokemon like Celesteela and Flare Boost Flutter Mane. Clear Smog, Topsy-Turvy, and Glare prevent Blissey from being setup fodder, with the latter being particularly useful against Magic Guard Eternatus and Imposter users, while Stone Axe provides utility if setup control is not necessary. Assault Vest further amplifies Blissey's Special Defense against powerful wallbreakers like Mega Alakazam that could otherwise outdamage Regenerator healing, while Heavy-Duty Boots prevents entry hazard damage and allows Blissey to access the aforementioned status moves. Blissey pairs incredibly well with very fast offensive Pokemon like Mega Alakazam and Mega Sceptile, as it provides slow pivoting, Wish support to offset residual damage for Mega Alakazam and allow Mega Sceptile to forgo Strength Sap, and healing denial against special walls like Ice Scales Arceus formes, while they revenge-kill numerous threats that Blissey can pivot into, especially those that Blissey cannot beat by itself such as Eternatus and Flare Boost Flutter Mane. Strong physical attackers also work well, as they can offensively Imposter-proof Blissey without conceding momentum; particularly, Ash-Greninja can pick off chipped Ice Scales Arceus formes. Blissey cannot handle certain special attackers like Psystrike Mega Alakazam, Shell Side Arm Eternatus, and various Secret Sword users, mandating a secondary special wall like Ice Scales users. Depending on the moveset, Blissey can be Imposter-proofed by other RegenVest users or Magic Guard users.

Magic Guard.description: Mega Sceptile is a decent fast attacker. Its main draw is its high Speed, giving it the jump on other common fast attackers like Ash-Greninja, Eternatus, and after chip damage, Flutter Mane. Life Orb-boosted Dragon Energy and Chloroblast pack a good punch, letting Mega Sceptile threaten neutral targets like Mega Audino, and Chloroblast uniquely matches up well against Mega Swampert and Arceus-Ground. Torch Song targets Steel-types like Celesteela and Magearna, and it can also enable late-game sweeps against softened teams. Mega Sceptile best fits on balance teams looking for a tool to out-offense traditional fast attackers like Eternatus without having to deal with a Choice lock like Mega Alakazam does. Mega Sceptile's damage isn't particularly high, even after Torch Song, and Strength Sap with only special moves means Strength Sap blockers like Magic Guard Blissey are a big problem; for this reason, Mega Sceptile should be used as a supporting piece for other special wallbreakers like Flutter Mane and Secret Sword Arceus-Ghost. Primordial Sea Steel-types like Registeel are also essentially immune to damage from Mega Sceptile, so Magic Guard users that can switch in and cut their utility are recommended. Mega Sceptile is frail and also needs to keep its Life Orb to deal any acceptable damage, so slow pivots like Mega Audino are essentially mandatory for it. Imposter-proof Mega Sceptile with a special wall like Magic Guard Blissey or Imposter Chansey.

RegenVest.description: RegenVest Primal Kyogre is a useful special wall, pivot, and utility Pokemon, notably checking foes like Flutter Mane, Palkia-O, and Eternatus. Flip Turn allows Primal Kyogre to safely scout moves from opposing threats like Choice Specs Flutter Mane and then swap into the appropriate answer, be it an Imposter Pokemon or an Ice Scales user. Scald is typically preferred over Steam Eruption thanks to its higher PP, but some teams may appreciate the extra power of Steam Eruption. Knock Off cripples item-reliant Pokemon like Chansey, RegenVest Dialga, and RegenVest Celesteela along with removing Covert Cloak to let it status Pokemon it otherwise couldn't, such as Mega Audino. Mortal Spin's entry hazard removal and poison-inflicting capabilities make it a good all-around utility choice, especially since Primal Kyogre has an excellent matchup against many of the metagame's Steel-types, like Mega Steelix and Celesteela. Nuzzle can slow down wallbreakers like Eternatus and Choice Specs Flutter Mane, and Stone Axe can help wear down foes for allies. Primal Kyogre's fear of physical wallbreakers like Mega Garchomp, Ash-Greninja, and Ultra Necrozma makes reliable checks to them, like Fur Coat Mega Audino and Ice Scales Arceus-Ground, useful teammates. Special wallbreakers like Flutter Mane, Eternatus, and Miraidon appreciate Knock Off removing items from opposing RegenVest users and Ability Shields from Pokemon like Ice Scales Arceus-Ground. Imposter-proof Primal Kyogre with a Take Heart Arceus forme holding a Plate.

Garchomp-Mega.overview: Mega Garchomp is one of the tier's most powerful wallbreakers thanks to its exceptionally high Attack, great STAB coverage, and strong STAB moves. Its raw power practically mandates a sturdy Fur Coat user to check it, as even Celesteela, which at least resists both STAB types, is 2HKOed by Glaive Rush without Fur Coat. The excellent coverage from its STAB moves also allows it to have good flexibility with its remaining moveslots to snipe checks, and Mega Garchomp also in particular has a strong non-contact STAB move in Precipice Blades. Mega Garchomp also resists Stealth Rock, which allows it to enter the field more often and help differentiate it from other wallbreakers like Mega Blaziken and Mega Gardevoir. It is also able to run a setup set that can sweep weakened teams with the boosted Speed. However, Mega Garchomp has a very middling Speed stat without boosts, which leaves it outsped by several prominent walls, such as the Arceus formes and Zamazenta, mandating teammates that can prevent Strength Sap healing from them. It also means it is vulnerable to revenge killing from every other offensive Pokemon, such as Flutter Mane, Eternatus, and Mega Gardevoir. The setup sets lose out on some initial power and miss out on an often crucial coverage move. Mega Garchomp's STAB moves, while having good coverage, also both have fairly common immunities, requiring correct predictions.
Garchomp-Mega.comments:
Other Options

Choice Band sets can opt for a Jolly nature to outspeed certain Pokemon like neutral-nature Mega Blaziken and Yveltal, but the loss of power is notable against checks like Fur Coat Arceus formes. Setup sets can have some deviations. Swords Dance + Speed Boost trades lower initial power for boosting faster than Strength Sap's Attack drops and outspeeding Imposter users that are pivoted in, while Flame Orb + Guts provides pseudo status immunity and Knock Off resilience in return for lower damage output and residual damage.

Checks and Counters

Fur Coat: Fur Coat users that are not weak to Mega Garchomp's STAB moves or V-create can stave it off in the short term even if their healing from Strength Sap is denied, especially with Leftovers. Shore Up users can be particularly problematic, since they can handle Mega Garchomp for much longer due to the move not being blockable, requiring it to PP stall out their recovery.

Faster Pokemon: Mega Garchomp's low Speed tier means a majority of the tier can outspeed it. Offensive Pokemon like Mega Gardevoir and Eternatus threaten to OHKO it, while defensive Pokemon like Yveltal and the Arceus formes neutralize it with moves like Knock Off and Strength Sap.

Ground-immune Pokemon: While they fear V-create, as well as Bolt Strike particularly in Celesteela's case, Celesteela and Levitate Steel-types like Arceus-Steel and Melmetal resist Dragon and are immune to Ground. Regenerator Celesteela can scout the Choice Band set's Choice lock, while Fire-immune sets do not fear V-create and can sacrifice itself to burn Mega Garchomp with Beak Blast. These Pokemon also can answer the setup set depending on coverage.

Pixilate: Pixilate users like Iron Valiant and Mega Gardevoir carry Extreme Speed, which can revenge kill Shift Gear and Speed Boost Mega Garchomp variants after Headlong Rush's Defense drops or with Glaive Rush's effect.

Choice Band.description:
Precipice Blades is less accurate than Headlong Rush and requires Sword of Ruin, which hits Imposter users for less damage due to the ability cancelling out, but it is valuable as a non-contact move to avoid Rocky Helmet and moves like Burning Bulwark; Headlong Rush should be used with Tough Claws. V-create is the best option to hit Fur Coat Celesteela and Levitate Steel-types like Arceus-Steel and Melmetal. Sunsteel Strike allows Mega Garchomp to OHKO Fur Coat Mega Audino and Arceus-Fairy after Stealth Rock, while Bolt Strike 2HKOes Fur Coat Mega Slowbro and Dondozo. First Impression offers useful priority that can let Mega Garchomp threaten faster Pokemon like Ultra Necrozma and Mega Alakazam, though other priority moves like Extreme Speed or Shadow Sneak are also options depending on desired targets.

Mega Garchomp is most commonly seen on slower balance teams that can repeatedly pivot it in to gradually break down opposing teams. Thus, slow pivots, including RegenVest users like Dialga-O and Teleport users like Blissey, are excellent to safely bring it in. Mega Garchomp's slow Speed mandates teammates that can deny Strength Sap healing from its checks; Chansey and Blissey can block Strength Sap from most Fur Coat Arceus formes, but they cannot handle other users like Zamazenta and Secret Sword users, making Pokemon like utility Flutter Mane and Magic Bounce Clodsire or Arceus-Poison more reliable. Mega Garchomp also can pair well with Doom Desire and Future Sight users like Dialga-O and Mega Slowbro, with the former threatening Fairy-types and the latter hitting Zamazenta. Paralysis support from Pokemon such as the aforementioned RegenVest users can be helpful to slow down fast walls like Fur Coat Zamazenta as well as Imposter users. Mega Garchomp also appreciates Knock Off users removing items like Leftovers and Rocky Helmet. Imposter users can be useful to scout for Burning Bulwark and Beak Blast, and they also can take advantage of Fur Coat users that use Shore Up over Strength Sap. Thanks to its high damage output, Mega Garchomp can in return offensively Imposter-proof Pokemon weak to its STAB moves, such as the aforementioned Dialga-O, Blissey, and Poison-types. Mega Garchomp can be Imposter-proofed by most Fur Coat users that are not weak to its moves, such as the Arceus formes, Zamazenta, and Yveltal.

Setup Sweeper.description:
Shift Gear helps patch up Mega Garchomp's low Speed, and, unlike Tidy Up, it allows Adamant Mega Garchomp to outspeed Mega Alakazam after a boost. Precipice Blades is an option over Headlong Rush to avoid Burning Bulwark mind games. Bolt Strike and Electro Drift hit numerous targets, notably Celesteela and Fur Coat Mega Slowbro, with Bolt Strike being stronger against Levitate Steel-types, while Electro Drift OHKOes Mega Slowbro and Fur Coat Celesteela with the Life Orb boost. Alternatively, V-create also hits Celesteela while OHKOing Levitate Steel-types like Arceus-Steel but has the drawback of reducing Mega Garchomp's Speed, leaving it more susceptible to being revenge killed. Tough Claws should be used with Headlong Rush or Electro Drift, while Sword of Ruin should be used with Precipice Blades. Life Orb offers a crucial damage bonus, letting Mega Garchomp hit benchmarks like OHKOing Ho-Oh and Primal Kyogre unboosted as well as OHKOing non-Fur Coat Arceus formes at +1. 132 Defense EVs is an option for Tough Claws sets to ensure that Eviolite Imposter is always OHKOed at +1.

Setup Mega Garchomp has limited longevity considering its reliance on Life Orb, making it more suited to be used as a cleaner when opposing walls have been conditioned to be broken through. Mega Garchomp can soften up walls for itself in the earlier stages, but support from teammates is necessary due to its lack of sustain. Teammates that can greatly limit Strength Sap healing like Chansey, Blissey, and Magic Bounce users such as Arceus-Poison and Clodsire are almost mandatory to keep physical walls chipped, and in return Mega Garchomp serves as a decent offensive Imposter-proof for the mentioned Pokemon. Entry hazard support from the likes of Arceus-Ghost, Flutter Mane, and RegenVest users also help chip Fur Coat Pokemon into range of being KOed. RegenVest users can also provide Knock Off and Nuzzle support; the latter is particularly useful to neutralize Imposter users and provide potential setup opportunities. Mega Garchomp can be Imposter-proofed by most Fur Coat users that carry Strength Sap or Topsy-Turvy and are not weak to its moves, such as Arceus-Fairy, Zamazenta, Yveltal, and if it is not running an Electric-type move, Water-types like Mega Slowbro and Dondozo.

Nasty Plot.description: Palkia-O is a strong setup sweeper that has an unremovable item and a strong matchup against the likes of Arceus-Ground and Ho-Oh. Speed Boost denies offensive counterplay from Pokemon like Ash-Greninja and Flutter Mane, and it prevents slow pivots like Teleport Blissey from forcing Palkia-O out via switching to Imposter users. Magic Guard can exploit defensive Pokemon that rely on status moves to deal damage, lets Palkia-O sit on common Knock Off users, and helps keep Dragon Energy's power high. Jungle Healing provides some recovery and removes debilitating status conditions like paralysis, but Strength Sap can provide much greater amount of healing. Pokemon like Ultra Necrozma and Mega Gardevoir appreciate Palkia-O beating Ho-Oh and Arceus-Ground, and they can help it beat checks like Mega Audino and Blissey in return. Speed Boost Palkia-O essentially ceases to function if statused, so a status absorber like a Take Heart Arceus forme can help. Teammates such as Blissey that can take on faster attackers like Eternatus and Flutter Mane are also greatly appreciated by Magic Guard variants. Imposter-proof Palkia-O with Ice Scales Mega Audino, Arceus-Fairy, or Imposter Chansey.

Defensive.description: Arceus-Electric's main draw is its Electric typing, which grants it both STAB on Thunder Cage and a resistance to it, giving Arceus-Electric a niche over other mostly neutral options like Arceus. Ice Beam provides generally good coverage, hitting Dragon- and Ground-types like Eternatus and Mega Garchomp that can otherwise switch into Thunder Cage safely. Secret Sword is an option that lets Arceus-Electric bypass Blissey, an otherwise hard stop to it that also denies Strength Sap healing. Ice Scales variants can struggle to answer powerful threats like Mega Alakazam and Flutter Mane long-term, making a secondary specially defensive teammate, such as a RegenVest user or Blissey, helpful. Fur Coat variants can handle most physical threats but are notably weak to Mega Garchomp, making a teammate like Celesteela or a secondary Fur Coat user necessary. Arceus-Electric usually can Imposter-proof itself, but Pokemon like Imposter Chansey can act as a secondary Imposter-proofer if it is weakened or inflicted with Salt Cure.

Arceus-Fairy.overview: Arceus-Fairy is a great, flexible, defensive option for most teams thanks to its typing, good bulk that allows it to serve a role as either a Fur Coat or an Ice Scales Pokemon, and high Speed that eclipses most physical attackers for the Fur Coat set. Its bulk also makes it a solid choice for a bulky Take Heart sweeper, with Thunder Cage + Strength Sap allowing it to beat most Imposter users one-on-one. A mixed set with Pixilate allows Arceus-Fairy to scare potential checks and counters with a powerful Extreme Speed. Arceus-Fairy's main flaw is arguably its dependence on Strength Sap for healing, which means Magic Guard Blissey and Chansey are perfect counters, since they have the perfect kits to freely switch into Arceus-Fairy and stall it out; Arceus-Fairy can't reliably use Recover instead, as doing so completely forfeits its advantage against Impostor users. Additionally, Fur Coat Arceus-Fairy can struggle against common Steel-type coverage from Choice Band users like Mega Garchomp, while Ice Scales variants are vulnerable to Eternatus and super effective coverage like Noxious Torque Ash-Greninja and Gigaton Hammer Mega Sceptile.
Arceus-Fairy.comments:
Other Options

Heavy-Duty Boots serves as a good option for Arceus-Fairy in general, as preventing entry hazard damage can end up being important in the long run. Covert Cloak is also a viable option, preventing a lot of chip damage from Salt Cure. Arceus-Fairy can run a more support-oriented moveset with moves such as Spikes, but this makes it quite challenging to Impostor-proof.

Checks and Counters

Blissey and Chansey: Their immense bulk and Magic Guard render Take Heart Arceus-Fairy almost helpless against them, and their tiny Attack means Arceus-Fairy heals for basically nothing with Strength Sap.

Ice Scales: Other Ice Scales users, such as various Arceus forms, Meloetta, and Mega Audino, can check Take Heart Arceus-Fairy, though they should be wary of Thunder Cage's residual damage.

Steel-types: Strong Steel-type wallbreakers such as Mega Lucario greatly threaten Arceus-Fairy. Defensively, Pokemon like Solgaleo and Arceus-Steel can check all variants of Arceus-Fairy.

Salt Cure: Unless Covert Cloak is used, Salt Cure puts Take Heart Arceus-Fairy on a timer, making it more difficult to set up, as well as making it so Arceus-Fairy is unable to Impostor-proof itself.

Eternatus: Eternatus can pressure unboosted Arceus-Fairy out with Poison-type moves. Unaware Eternatus can switch in on any Arceus-Fairy and use it as setup fodder.

Take Heart.description:
Take Heart provides boosting while denying poison from Mortal Spin, which would otherwise force it out. The Fairy-type move of choice can vary: Moonblast is the 2nd strongest Fairy move of the bunch, has higher PP, and has a decent Special Attack drop, while Revelation Dance is effective against Normalize. Leftovers allows Arceus-Fairy to not have its healing cut off by Pokemon with low attack, as well as giving it increased longevity.

Arceus-Fairy is typically splashed onto bulkier teams as a wall against threats like Mega Garchomp, Mega Sceptile, and Ash-Greninja, in addition to compressing a late-game win condition at the same time. Arceus-Fairy's solid typing and bulk mean it can serve as a good Imposter-proof to Pokemon like these. Arceus-Fairy does falter to fast Pokemon that can easily slot Steel or Poison-type coverage, like Koraidon for Fur Coat sets, and Sheer Force Ash-Greninja for Ice Scales sets, so defensive Pokemon that can stonewall them like RegenVest Dialga-O, Mega Swampert, and Zamazenta can assist with that, and, in Mega Swampert's case, provides a very valuable check to the threatening Mega Lucario. Arceus-Fairy is both stonewalled and has its healing denied by Blissey and Chansey, so a teammate with Psystrike or a similar move that can pressure them out, like Mega Gardevoir or High Jump Kick Mega Sceptile, is highly recommended.

Arceus-Ghost.overview: Arceus-Ghost is one of the most defining centerpieces of Balanced Hackmons thanks to its built-in self Imposter-proofing due to Spooky Plate + Judgment that allows it to run Shore Up without worrying about Imposter, good bulk, great offensive typing, and versatility. It is able to soft check a lot of would-be annoyances such as RegenVest Celesteela without Nuzzle and Mega Lucario depending on its ability. Its ability to block Rapid Spin also differentiates it from other Arceus formes. However, Arceus-Ghost can be hard walled rather easily by certain walls such as Mega Audino and Meloetta. Its low immediate damage output can also lead to fast offensive threats like Eternatus forcing it out. Its ability choice of Magic Guard or Fur Coat also mean it either has limited defensive presence or is a physical wall that's weak to Choice Band Ash-Greninja, respectively.
Arceus-Ghost.comments:
Other Options

Thunder Cage can constantly chip down most bulkier walls and hits Celesteela hard, but Mortal Spin is generally better at chipping down walls on utility sets, which leaves Thunder Cage mostly just for Celesteela. Other offensive abilities such as Speed Boost and Good as Gold can be chosen over Fur Coat on the setup set, but this provides little defensive utility and would leave Arceus-Ghost with fewer opportunities to set up. Taunt is an option on the setup set to deny recovery and other utility such as Spikes and Topsy-Turvy, but giving up a moveslot for utility such as Knock Off or coverage is often not desirable. Plus, it either locks Arceus-Ghost into using Judgment as its only attack or means it has no recovery, both of which are very easily exploitable.

Checks and Counters

Dark-types: Arceus-Ghost struggles against most Dark-type foes in general, especially faster attackers such as Ash-Greninja. However, they dislike switching in on Secret Sword or Knock Off especially with entry hazards up. Magic Guard Yveltal in particular, however, is a great annoyance, as it completely halts anything Arceus-Ghost can do.

Normal-type Walls: Blissey and Mega Audino handily beat setup sets via Topsy-Turvy and their immunity to Judgment, although Blissey needs to watch out for Secret Sword. Utility sets are hard stopped by Magic Guard Blissey, and even if it is not running Magic Guard, Arceus-Ghost can only make progress through Knock Off and Spikes.

Faster Threats: Most faster threats, like Flutter Mane, Eternatus, and Mega Alakazam, threaten Arceus-Ghost out with direct damage. They can't really switch in, however.

Status Effects: Fur Coat Arceus-Ghost sets hate status, as chip damage from poison cuts heavily into its longevity, and paralysis completely cripples it. Magic Guard sets don't mind poison but still dislike paralysis.

Ice Scales + Take Heart Arceus Formes: Ice Scales Acreus amplified with Take Heart's Special Defense boosts means that all of Arceus-Ghost's attacks, including Secret Sword, will do basically no damage to these Arceus formes. Many of them also carry a Plate, which means they can easily sponge Knock Off.

Utility.description:
Arceus-Ghost can provide very consistent utility to its team with its immunity to item removal and built in self-Imposter-proofing through Judgment. Shore Up is the preferred healing move because it isn't blocked by the likes of Blissey and Mega Audino, but Strength Sap heals more HP at once and fares better against Choice Band users. Knock Off removes crucial items from walls like Heavy-Duty Boots and Covert Cloak from Mega Audino, making them susceptible to entry hazard damage or getting poisoned by Mortal Spin. Potentially removing Eviolite from Imposter users is also nice but should not be relied on, as these foes rarely want to switch in on Arceus-Ghost. Spikes takes advantage of Arceus-Ghost's conventional switch-ins, like Blissey, being passive and lacking hazard removal; alternatively, Rapid Spin and Mortal Spin provide hard-to-block hazard removal. Magic Guard is the preferred ability, as it provides an immunity to entry hazards and other chip damage such as Salt Cure and Mortal Spin, but Fur Coat makes Arceus-Ghost function as more of a wall capable of soft checking foes like Koraidon.

Arceus-Ghost fits well on entry hazard-stacking teams, as it can easily provide item removal support and reliably block Rapid Spin. Teams that stack hazard-immune teammates also enjoy Arceus-Ghost as a Knock Off absorber. Most attackers such as Ash-Greninja or and Eternatus appreciate Arceus-Ghost stacking Spikes to wear away at their checks or, in Eternatus's case, directly damage checks like Ice Scales Arceus formes. Other teammates such as RegenVest Celesteela and Mega Audino also synergize well with Arceus-Ghost defensively alongside the occasional Imposter-proofing Arceus-Ghost can provide for them. Arceus-Ghost is particularly vulnerable to Dark-types and opposing Ghost-types that are faster such as Ash-Greninja, Yveltal, and Flutter Mane; therefore, checks to them like the aforementioned Mega Audino are valuable.

Setup Sweeper.description:
Setup Arceus-Ghost acts as a fairly dangerous Imposter-proof sweeper that can also function as a bulky physical wall. Shore Up provides consistent recovery and isn't stopped by Strength Sap blockers like Blissey and Mega Audino, the latter of which Arceus-Ghost can't feasibly threaten; alternatively, Strength Sap has higher instant healing and makes it much more solid against physical attackers and Imposter users. Nasty Plot provides an immediately threatening boost, letting Arceus-Ghost immediately 2HKO neutral non-Ice Scales Arceus formes without any prior chip damage. Torch Song boosts slower but damages while boosting and provides a strong tool against RegenVest Celesteela. Take Heart bolsters Arceus-Ghost's special bulk and gets rid of crippling status such as paralysis from Glare and poison from Mortal Spin, but it is not advised if not using Strength Sap or Knock Off, as Imposter users can wall Arceus-Ghost barring critical hits. Knock Off is the preferred last move, as it removes crucial items such as Heavy-Duty Boots from the likes of Mega Audino and Assault Vest from Celesteela. Secret Sword is an alternative that provides no support but ensures Arceus-Ghost can break through Blissey and certain Take Heart Arceus formes such as Ice Scales Arceus-Electric if running Take Heart itself.

Arceus-Ghost best functions as both a sturdy physical wall early-game and a late-game wincon when most of its checks have been chipped down or crippled, so it pairs well with early- to mid-game wallbreakers or defensive progress-makers, such as Mega Garchomp and Spikes users like Mega Audino; Arceus-Ghost can in return help Imposter-proof them or block Rapid Spin. Arceus-Ghost enjoys entry hazard support in particular, as it forces a lot of switches after setting up, forcing quite a bit of free chip damage. Arceus-Ghost is mostly checked by Mega Audino, so another teammate that can pressure it such as Eternatus is also helpful. Arceus-Ghost should not be used as your only physical wall, as it is very vulnerable to Choice Band Ash-Greninja, and Shore Up sets can be prone to running out of PP in longer games; therefore, another physical wall such as Fur Coat Mega Audino or Fur Coat Zacian pairs well with it.

Arceus-Steel.overview: Arceus-Steel serves as a powerful hybrid Pokemon, capable of checking threats like Flutter Mane and Mega Gardevoir while sporting good offensive presence. A particularly distinguishing trait of Arceus-Steel is its ease of self-Imposter proofing as a setup Pokemon while still possessing good coverage and not being dependent on Strength Sap. Its Steel-type STAB moves are also valuable for being super effective on Mega Audino, the most common Ice Scales user, as well as utility Flutter Mane, which annoys most special Arceus formes. Additionally, Arceus-Steel has numerous viable variations in its ability and Plate, which allow it to circumvent specific checks or offer additional defensive utility. Arceus-Steel's typing does leave it susceptible to the most common wallbreakers in Mega Garchomp and Mega Blaziken, as well as Fire-type coverage from the likes of Eternatus and Mega Sceptile should it not have Well-Baked Body. This, combined with it also taking up the Arceus forme slot, means its teammates will be tasked with handling many dominant wallbreakers. It is also weak to Fighting-type moves from popular Pokemon like Zamazenta, and Steel is only a mediocre offensive type, being resisted by Pokemon such as Ho-Oh, Miraidon, and opposing Steel-types.
Arceus-Steel.comments:
Other Options

Arceus-Steel can run a variety of utility abilities to deny counterplay options or provide additional defensive capabilities. Magic Bounce is a close alternative to Good as Gold, being able to block most debilitating status moves; it notably is useful against Spikes and Strength Sap, but in return, it cannot block Pain Split and leaves Arceus-Steel susceptible to being forced out by Parting Shot. Shield Dust blocks Nuzzle and notably Salt Cure; Magic Guard also stops Salt Cure, as well as Spikes damage; Motor Drive offers an immunity to Nuzzle, Thunder Cage, and Volt Switch; and Purifying Salt grants a status immunity and allows Arceus-Steel to check offensive Flutter Mane sets. Steely Spirit is an option to greatly increase Arceus-Steel's damage output, allowing it to almost OHKO Zamazenta at +2 with Tachyon Cutter. Arceus-Steel can run an Ice Scales set with moves like Topsy-Turvy to serve as a sturdier check against threats like Mega Gardevoir and Eternatus, but this set faces competition from Well-Baked Body variants and is not self-Imposter-proof.

Checks and Counters

Salt Cure: Unless Arceus-Steel has Shield Dust or Magic Guard, it is highly susceptible to Salt Cure from Pokemon such as RegenVest Celesteela and Primal Kyogre, effectively forcing it out.

Fire-type Pokemon and coverage: Ho-Oh and Mega Blaziken threaten Arceus-Steel with V-create; the former resists every common move Arceus-Steel runs and can even wall Well-Baked Body variants if it carries Topsy-Turvy, while the latter forces out even Well-Baked Body variants, though it needs to be cautious of Earth Plate variants. Pokemon that Arceus-Steel normally checks, such as Mega Gardevoir and Eternatus, can also hit it hard with Fire-type coverage like Armor Cannon and Torch Song.

Fighting-type Pokemon: Zamazenta and Koraidon outspeed Arceus-Steel, have enough bulk to survive +2 Tachyon Cutter, and threaten it with super effective STAB moves.

Paralysis: Arceus-Steel loses a lot of its offensive potential if it gets paralyzed. It also fails to reliably check Pokemon such as Mega Gardevoir once paralyzed.

Electric-type Pokemon: Electric-type Pokemon resist Steel and are not weak to Fighting. Miraidon outspeeds Arceus-Steel and can threaten it with Encore or Torch Song, though it needs to be wary of Earth Plate Judgment, while Arceus-Electric is almost always has Ice Scales and wins the matchup with Take Heart and Thunder Cage.

Ground-type Pokemon: Mega Garchomp survives +2 Tachyon Cutter and OHKOes Arceus-Steel with its Ground-type move or V-create. Mega Swampert and the rare Ice Scales Arceus-Ground can take hits comfortably and retaliate hard against non-Levitate variants.

Nasty Plot.description:
With a natural immunity to Mortal Spin, as well as self-Imposter-proofing potential by resisting Imposter users' Judgment, Arceus-Steel is free to use Nasty Plot, considerably improving its offensive capabilities and turning it into a potent bulky setup attacker. This also means it can run Shore Up over Strength Sap while still beating Imposter users, allowing it to avoid having its healing denied from low-Attack Pokemon like Chansey and Magic Bounce users. Tachyon Cutter is the STAB move of choice, with its multiple hits also being useful in setup wars for higher critical hit chances, but Revelation Dance should be used on non-Good as Gold sets that need to hit Normalize users. Judgment allows Arceus-Steel to Imposter-proof itself while running a Plate of a type that is super effective against transformed Imposter users. Fist Plate is generally the go-to option, providing coverage on Steel-types, as well as neutral coverage on Pokemon such as Celesteela, while hitting Ting-Lu and Chansey. Alternatively, Earth Plate is an option on non-Levitate sets, still hitting Steel-types like Registeel and opposing Arceus-Steel but also hitting Mega Blaziken, Miraidon, and Eternatus super effectively, notably preventing the former from coming in to force Arceus-Steel out. Good as Gold blocks a myriad of status moves that could otherwise neuter Arceus-Steel, including Topsy-Turvy, Encore, and Parting Shot. It also denies Flutter Mane's healing from Pain Split, Entrainment from Normalize users, and Strength Sap users in a pinch. Alternatively, Well-Baked Body lets Arceus-Steel answer Torch Song Dragon-types and flip the matchup completely on some Ho-Oh sets. Levitate is another option, particularly for entry hazard stack teams, as it provides an immunity to Spikes while letting Arceus-Steel switch into Mega Garchomp's STAB moves.

Arceus-Steel can fit on most playstyles due to its flexibility. Offensive and balance teams appreciate its role compression in defensive utility and offensive presence, while Levitate variants fit well on entry hazard stack structures. Knock Off support from the likes of Ting-Lu, Zamazenta, and Mega Gardevoir is highly appreciated, as they can remove key items such as Celesteela's Assault Vest and Ho-Oh's Heavy-Duty Boots, preventing them from checking Arceus-Steel effectively. Residual damage sources from Spikes and Stone Axe are also helpful to chip checks into KO range, particularly offensive checks like Mega Garchomp and Mega Blaziken. Flutter Mane is notable as a Spikes setter, since it can be Imposter-proofed by Arceus-Steel and also act as a switch-in to Zamazenta depending on the ability. Other checks to Zamazenta include utility Eternatus and Fur Coat Celesteela, with the former also providing a Fire resistance and the latter a Ground immunity. Arceus-Steel can Imposter-proof certain special wallbreakers, such as Knock Off Mega Gardevoir and, if it is running Well-Baked Body, Mega Sceptile and Eternatus; in return, they help apply pressure on shared special walls like Chansey, Ho-Oh, and RegenVest Pokemon. Arceus-Steel is generally self-Imposter-proof, but teammates like Chansey can easily serve as secondary Imposter-proof answers should it end up too low on health.

Defensive.description: Arceus-Water differentiates itself from other bulky Water-types such as Mega Slowbro and Dondozo through its good Speed tier and balanced bulk, which makes it more resilient to slower wallbreakers like Mega Garchomp. Its Special Defense also means it doesn't instantly fold to Electro Drift coverage, while the aforementioned Water-types are both OHKOed by any Tough Claws user with it. Fur Coat allows Arceus-Water to wall threats like Mega Garchomp and Ash-Greninja, albeit needing a Bold nature to dodge a potential 2HKO from Ash-Greninja's Flower Trick. Scald threatens burns on Pokemon like Mega Lucario and defensive switch-ins like RegenVest users. Due to Arceus-Water's weakness to Thunder Cage, Spirit Shackle is the safest option for Fur Coat variants to Imposter-proof themselves. Spirit Shackle variants can utilize Shore Up to not have recovery denied by Magic Guard Blissey. Arceus-Water still takes a lot of damage from coverage options like Electro Drift and Ash-Greninja's Flower Trick, so backup checks to these like Dragon-types and Fur Coat Fairy-types help. Arceus-Water can generally Imposter-proof itself but can always be Imposter-proofed by Imposter Chansey as a backup.

Mega Audino utilizes its very rare typing, good bulk, low Attack capable of stifling Strength Sap healing, and low Speed to be a versatile wall and an effective pivot. The ability chosen is up to team preference; Fur Coat can let it check Dragon- and Dark-type physical attackers like Mega Garchomp and Choice Band Ash-Greninja, Ice Scales can let it check various special threats like Flutter Mane and Take Heart Arceus formes, and Magic Bounce can reflect key status moves like Spikes, Glare, Strength Sap, and Toxic. Topsy-Turvy heavily deters setup from threats like Flare Boost Flutter Mane, Take Heart Arceus formes, and Mega Garchomp. Knock Off removes key items such as Assault Vest from Mega Steelix; if consistent progress making throughout the game is desired, Mortal Spin can instead be used to remove entry hazards and spread poison. Teleport is a consistent pivoting option with the switches Mega Audino often forces by denying Strength Sap healing or threatening to remove items. Moonblast hits Magic Guard Miraidon for good damage and dissuades other Magic Guard users from freely switching in. Mega Audino fits on balance or bulky teams looking for a flexible check to most of the metagame, a Pokemon with a Ghost-type immunity, or more importantly a Strength Sap blocker; thus, strong physical attackers like Ash-Greninja and Kyurem-B are good partners that appreciate its ability to limit Strength Sap healing and slow pivoting. In return, Mega Audino can Imposter-proof these attackers. Mega Audino is weak to Sunsteel Strike users like Mega Lucario, so solid checks to them like Mega Slowbro and Celesteela makes for good partners. Imposter-proof Mega Audino with Steel-type teammates, Regenerator users, or Take Heart Arceus formes carrying a Plate.

Choice Band.description: Kyurem-B's main niche compared to competitors like Mega Garchomp and Ash-Greninja lies in its immensely spammable Glacial Lance, which hits most physical walls for at least neutral damage while also being non-contact to avoid Rocky Helmet and Burning Bulwark. Bolt Strike 2HKOes Fur Coat Water-types like Mega Slowbro and Dondozo in addition to Primordial Sea Registeel. U-turn eases prediction and chips walls like Mega Slowbro. Headlong Rush and V-create both target Steel-types like Zacian-C, with the former OHKOing Primordial Sea Registeel and the latter OHKOing Mega Steelix. Kyurem-B's main weakness is its vulnerability to Stealth Rock, making strong entry hazard removal mandatory. Due to Glacial Lance's excellent neutral coverage and Kyurem-B's raw power, it forces most physical walls to remain healthy to avoid being KOed the next time it attacks, making Strength Sap blockers like Magic Guard Blissey and Magic Bounce users fantastic teammates. Kyurem-B is also very slow for a wallbreaker, making fast Pokemon such as Ash-Greninja and Koraidon desirable. Imposter-proof Kyurem-B with Fur Coat Pokemon such as Arceus-Ghost and Mega Audino.

Greninja-Ash.overview: Ash-Greninja's high Speed and solid offensive stats make it an effective attacker. STAB-boosted Surging Strikes and Wicked Blow are excellent options for Choice Band sets, taking advantage of good Attack and Sniper to power through physical walls and force out non-resistant foes. Alternatively, Sheer Force sets can circumvent common Dark-resistant checks, like Mega Audino and Arceus-Fairy, with special attacks like Steam Eruption or super effective coverage like Noxious Torque. Ash-Greninja's main issue is that it requires prediction to do significant damage; Rocky Helmet + Fur Coat users, most of which resist Dark, can mostly stop Sniper sets from doing meaningful damage until a Knock Off lands in the right place. Sheer Force sets are strong but not mind-bogglingly so, meaning constant predictions are necessary to keep up damage. Ash-Greninja also sits in an awkward Speed tier that commonly leaves it forced out by foes like Mega Sceptile and Flutter Mane. Both sets also lack recovery, meaning that entry hazards wear into Ash-Greninja's HP, especially given the Rocky Helmet issue.
Greninja-Ash.comments:
Other Options

Sniper sets can occasionally find use in Frost Breath, which gives Ash-Greninja an easier time dispatching Koraidon and other Fur Coat Dragon-types, but these are very seldom used, and Frost Breath is a terrible move to be locked into. Similarly, Tough Claws + Life Orb can be used in place of Sniper thanks to Electro Drift, which both hits Imposter users and dispatches Celesteela and Dondozo, but the damage drop on Ash-Greninja's physical attacks is less than desirable. Magic Guard is an option to retain health much more safely and get use out of Water Spout and Wave Crash, but the set still falters to Fur Coat Dragon-types. Ash-Greninja is also arguably the best rain sweeper with Tinted Lens + Water Spout, but this requires commitment of the entire team and is generally unreliable.

Checks and Counters

Rocky Helmet: Sniper sets are essentially stalled until Rocky Helmet is removed from physical walls, as most ones looking to check Ash-Greninja will resist Dark; if Ash-Greninja wants any instant damage with Surging Strikes, it costs 50% of its HP.

Faster Pokemon: Ash-Greninja's awkward Speed means that Koraidon, Zamazenta, and Miraidon can outspeed and defensively check it should they have Fur Coat. Offensive Pokemon used primarily for their Speed, like Mega Sceptile, Mega Alakazam, and Flutter Mane, can also all chase it out, but they can't switch in.

Strength Sap: While Surging Strikes and Wicked Blow ignore Strength Sap's Attack drops, Ash-Greninja struggles to KO neutral targets from full, and one of its STAB-boosted moves will usually be near-dead weight due to the opponent's Fur Coat user's typing. This limits Ash-Greninja's switch in opportunities and pressures its PP conservation. Sheer Force sets also similarly don't enjoy Bolt Strike and Combat Torque faltering against Ice Scales users.

Entry Hazards: Ash-Greninja's lack of recovery and already-high contact move usage mean entry hazards can largely keep it off the field.

Fur Coat: Fur Coat walls can dodge all 2HKOs from Sniper sets and PP stall them long-term without extensive entry hazard chip damage. Those faster than Ash-Greninja, like Zamazenta, are almost impossible to wear down. These dislike Sheer Force sets, however.

Sniper.description:
Sniper's immense damage buff makes Ash-Greninja a strong Choice Band user, with even the bulkiest physical walls like Fur Coat Arceus-Fairy taking 40% per attack. Flower Trick is largely an Imposter deterrent, but it helps out against the occasional Fur Coat Arceus-Water and Dondozo, albeit needing entry hazard support for the latter. Knock Off allows Ash-Greninja to remove Rocky Helmet for itself, which is its primary bottleneck for dealing damage in most games; removing Heavy-Duty Boots and Eviolite from foes like Mega Audino and Imposter users, respectively, also greatly helps.

Sniper Ash-Greninja is generally best on balance teams as the primary progress maker thanks to its commanding damage output. Slow pivot support is mandatory, as Ash-Greninja cannot afford to take Knock Off and severely dislikes status from moves like Mortal Spin and Glare; this usually comes in the form of RegenVest users, which also help via Stone Axe and Knock Off. A secondary physical wallbreaker can prove useful, as it's possible that Ash-Greninja can get hung up on walls chosen specifically to counter it, namely faster Dark-resistant foes like Koraidon and Zamazenta. Strong hazard removal is highly recommended due to Ash-Greninja's lack of recovery, with Mortal Spin's poison additionally helping to pick off frailer targets. Hazard support is useful, as Ash-Greninja's sheer power allows it to 2HKO most walls, as are allies that limit Strength Sap healing on these walls like Chansey and Blissey.

Primordial Sea.description: Primordial Sea Solgaleo is most notable for its ability to wall Eternatus, and it also serves as a solid defensive answer to threats like Ultra Necrozma, Desolate Land Mega Blaziken, and non-Headlong Rush Kyurem-B. Psychic Noise deals respectable damage against Eternatus and prevents it, along with potential switch-ins like Arceus formes, from using Strength Sap. Flip Turn is boosted by Primordial Sea and can deal minor chip damage, while Teleport provides slow pivoting despite Solgaleo's high Speed. Gigaton Hammer pressures Fairy-types like Mega Audino and dissuades Blissey from attempting to block Strength Sap. Spikes can help reduce Solgaleo's passivity against many defensive Pokemon. An Impish nature guarantees Solgaleo isn't 2HKOed by Sheer Force Ultra Necrozma's Bolt Strike, while Jolly allows Solgaleo to outrun Mega Blaziken and Mega Gardevoir. Solgaleo is useful when paired with threats like Ultra Necrozma, which usually require specific allies to Imposter-proof. Defensively, Solgaleo struggles against threats like Moongeist Beam users, Ash-Greninja, and Mega Garchomp, making walls like RegenVest Primal Kyogre and Fur Coat Fairy-types good teammates. Solgaleo can be Imposter-proofed by numerous options, but RegenVest users in particular do not care about Psychic Noise and can maintain momentum.

RegenVest.description: RegenVest Celesteela utilizes its excellent defensive typing to act as a reliable pivot capable of soft checking numerous threats, including Mega Garchomp, Flutter Mane, Tidy Up Arceus-Ground, and Take Heart Arceus formes. Knock Off provides excellent utility, nullifying offensive threats by removing items like Life Orb and Choice Specs while relieving defensive Pokemon of items like Covert Cloak and Leftovers. Stone Axe helps reduce Celesteela's passivity by providing entry hazard support for teammates. Salt Cure deters Pokemon like Take Heart Arceus formes from using Celesteela as setup bait, and it deals good chip damage to Imposter users. Doom Desire is an option to support offensive teammates against walls like Mega Audino. Beak Blast punishes moves such as U-turn and deters moves like Bolt Strike and Electro Drift. Even with Assault Vest, Celesteela's natural bulk is only decent, so it requires sturdy teammates like Ice Scales Mega Audino and Ho-Oh to pivot into to help against powerful wallbreakers like Choice Specs Flutter Mane and Mega Alakazam. Celesteela's typing also leaves it vulnerable to threats like Miraidon and Fire-type coverage from Pokemon such as Eternatus, making walls like Magic Guard Blissey desirable teammates. Imposter users also pair well with Celesteela to defeat Take Heart Arceus formes after they have been hit by Salt Cure. Imposter-proofing Celesteela can be annoying, with Take Heart Arceus formes and Magic Guard Pokemon that do not mind losing their item being decent options to do so.

Fur Coat.description: Fur Coat Celesteela acts as a decent physical wall for teams looking for a Ground immunity while soft checking Ash-Greninja and non-Mold Breaker Koraidon. Teleport brings in dangerous wallbreakers safely. Beak Blast, if it successfully burns, instantly kills any physical wallbreaker's hopes of dealing damage except mixed attackers with Electro Drift; it also outdamages Jungle Healing from Mega Garchomp. Knock Off annoys defensive switch-ins like Mega Audino by removing items like Covert Cloak, while Salt Cure can help rack up large amounts of passive damage and more directly threatens Imposter users. Stone Axe capitalizes on the switches Celesteela often forces by virtue of its typing by setting entry hazards, but it's notably worse against Imposter users. Recover provides reliable healing in the face of wallbreaker + Strength Sap blocker structures, while Strength Sap has higher burst healing against some teams, is easier to Imposter-proof, neutralizes Mega Garchomp, and helps versus Ash-Greninja. Rocky Helmet helps wear away at physical attackers without needing entry hazard support and throttles Ash-Greninja into spamming Wicked Blow, while Covert Cloak shuts off Salt Cure's crushing passive damage. Physical wallbreakers like Mega Garchomp appreciate Celesteela Imposter-proofing them and blocking Mortal Spin to keep hazards on the field to increase the odds of KOs. Celesteela can be vulnerable to Bolt Strike or Electro Drift users like Mega Lucario and mixed attackers like Ultra Necrozma, so sturdy walls to them like Miraidon and Ice Scales Arceus-Ground makes for good partners. Imposter-proof Celesteela with a Magic Bounce user, a Magic Guard user not deterred by burns or Knock Off like Arceus-Ghost, or a RegenVest user like Primal Kyogre.

Defensive.description: Zacian's niche lies in its excellent Speed, allowing it to outspeed threats like Ash-Greninja, Flutter Mane, and Ultra Necrozma and thus check them better than its competition like Arceus-Fairy. Fur Coat lets Zacian check threats like Ash-Greninja and Mega Garchomp, while Ice Scales lets it soft check Flutter Mane and Ultra Necrozma. Strength Sap is generally preferred for its higher healing output and useful Attack drops, but Shore Up can be used on Ice Scales sets to more reliably heal against low Attack Pokemon such as Mega Alakazam and Flutter Mane. Magical Torque's higher damage output is useful against Pokemon like Mega Garchomp and Mega Alakazam, but Spirit Break is more effective to stave off boosting special attackers like Nasty Plot Flutter Mane and non-Poison-type move Eternatus. Glare and Topsy-Turvy both serve as tools against setup, with the former more effective and disruptive but also more difficult to Imposter-proof and useless against Flare Boost Flutter Mane. Spikes and Stone Axe help reduce Zacian's passivity by providing entry hazard support. Leftovers is useful to reduce the frequency that Zacian needs to recover, while Rocky Helmet harshly punishes Ash-Greninja. Ability Shield is a niche choice that improves its matchup against Moongeist Beam and non-STAB-boosted Sunsteel Strike users. Fur Coat Zacian can struggle against certain physical attackers like Zacian-C and Mega Lucario, so teammates that can cover them such as Fur Coat Arceus-Electric and Mega Swampert are appreciated. Ice Scales Zacian has trouble with Eternatus and repeated attacks from Mega Alakazam and Flutter Mane, making secondary special walls like RegenVest Primal Kyogre good teammates. Zacian can be Imposter-proofed by Pokemon like Fur Coat Take Heart Arceus formes, Imposter Chansey, and Magic Bounce Ho-Oh.

Zamazenta.overview: Zamazenta is a good physical wall with its neutral typing against most physical threats, Stealth Rock resistance, decent bulk, and ability to outspeed many prominent physical attackers such as Ash-Greninja. Its servicable Attack and dangerous STAB moves such as Combat Torque and Triple Arrows also make Zamazenta less passive and actually capable of threatening certain walls out, also making it an excellent Mortal Spin user. Zamazenta's physical bulk is decent but not great, making it vulnerable to foes such as Mega Blaziken and Mega Garchomp. Zamazenta also struggles to deal any meaningful damage against foes that resist its Fighting-type STAB moves and can ignore poison from Mortal Spin like Magic Guard Arceus-Ghost or Eternatus, although it can still fish for a Combat Torque paralysis. In addition, Zamazenta is heavily prepared for by virtue of being the most versatile physical wall, which can lead to some consistency issues.
Zamazenta.comments:
Other Options

Shore Up is a usable option over Strength Sap to not be deterred by specific Magic Bounce users such as Arceus-Poison but is generally not worth it due to the fact that it heals less in many situations, is unable to deter setup, and Zamazenta does not have the raw bulk to afford the lower healing. U-turn is an option over Knock Off and can provide minor chip damage and pivoting, though the value of Knock Off is much more enticing than a fast pivot. Spirit Shackle is also an option to easily self-Imposter-proof, but again, the value Knock Off gives is just too valuable. Utility Umbrella lets Zamazenta check Desolate Land Mega Blaziken, though uncommon Mega Blaziken variants like Tough Claws can still blow Zamazenta over, and Utility Umbrella provides practically no other utility.

Checks and Counters

Ghost-types: Ghost-type foes such as Arceus-Ghost and Flutter Mane are immune to Zamazenta's STAB moves and are able to hit it hard. Many of them also commonly run Magic Guard or Covert Cloak, so Mortal Spin's poison will not affect them. However, every relevant Ghost-type is slower than Zamazenta, and they have to be aware of Knock Off's damage and item removal if said Ghost-type isn't Arceus-Ghost or Flare Boost Flutter Mane.

Poison-type Magic Bounce users: Poison-type Magic Bounce users like Arceus-Poison and Clodsire resist Zamazenta's Fighting-type moves and stop it from healing with Strength Sap while being immune to poison from Mortal Spin. Arceus-Poison can even rarely opt for a Plate, which thwarts Zamazenta's Knock Off.

Take Heart users: Take Heart users such as Arceus-Fairy and Arceus-Water can shrug off Mortal Spin's poison or Combat Torque's paralysis. Most of them also run Fur Coat and are slower than Zamazenta, which also means Zamazenta deals little damage while not being able to slowly chip them down with Mortal Spin.

Eternatus: Eternatus shrugs off any damage Zamazenta deals through its great bulk and Fighting resistance, though it has to mind entry hazard damage and Combat Torque's paralysis. Magic Guard sets additionally hate losing Life Orb, which makes it specifically a poor switch-in.

Mega Alakazam and Deoxys: Both of these foes are faster than Zamazenta and can OHKO it with their Psychic-type moves. They can also aggressively switch into Zamazenta sometimes, though their poor bulk means it should be treated as a last resort.

Physical attackers with super effective coverage: Certain attackers like Mega Aerodactyl and Ash-Greninja can KO Zamazenta with moves such as Brave Bird or Fleur Cannon.

Fur Coat.description:
Combat Torque is the most consistent STAB move thanks to its paralysis chance potentially crippling switch ins. Alternatively, Triple Arrows immediately threatens bulkier foes such as Ting-Lu with its Defense drop and flinch chance in exchange for the lack of paralysis in longer games. Mortal Spin synergies well with either of these moves, forcing out most Steel-type foes. Strength Sap is the preferred healing move thanks to its burst healing, which lets Zamazenta check certain threats such as Mega Garchomp; the difficulty that most Strength Sap blockers, such as Magic Guard Chansey, have switching in to Zamazenta; and its ability to neutralize physical setup. Knock Off removes key items such as Covert Cloak from Celesteela and Mega Audino. The item choice is up to preference: Covert Cloak blocks Mortal Spin and Salt Cure, while Heavy-Duty Boots prevents Spikes damage that would deter Zamazenta from removing entry hazards.

Zamazenta fits best on balance teams looking for a physical wall that is not passive and can answer most physical threats by itself; this is because of its neutrality to most physical foes alongside great Speed. Certain offensive teammates like Arceus-Ghost and Ultra Necrozma appreciate its natural speed control and ability to answer Ash-Greninja. Zamazenta can struggle with offensive threats that are too powerful such as Mega Blaziken and Mega Garchomp; therefore, soft checks to those threats like Mega Slowbro or a secondary Fur Coat user like Arceus-Fairy naturally make for good pairings. Imposter-proof Zamazenta with Fighting-resistant teammates that do not dislike poison like Magic Guard Arceus-Ghost or Eternatus.

Fur Coat Dondozo uses its massive physical bulk and solid typing to check powerful physical wallbreakers including Mega Garchomp, non-mixed Mega Lucario, and Ash-Greninja. Strength Sap is the preferred recovery option to also slow down setup sweepers like Mega Garchomp, but Shore Up cannot be blocked by Magic Bounce or be limited by Pokemon like Blissey. Dondozo's extremely low Speed also makes it a great pivot capable of underspeeding common defensive staples like Parting Shot Mega Audino. Glare cripples all physical attackers excluding Jungle Healing users as well as deterring most special wallbreakers, while Knock Off provides support by removing crucial items like Assault Vest. Stone Axe and Spikes help reduce Dondozo's passivity against Teleport users and Take Heart Arceus formes. Covert Cloak prevents annoying secondary effects from moves like Salt Cure and Mortal Spin, which is valuable because Dondozo can be 2HKOed by coverage moves after slight chip damage; alternatively, Rocky Helmet punishes otherwise safe U-turns and prevents foes like Mega Garchomp from simply PP stalling Dondozo's healing. Dondozo cannot answer mixed threats such as Electro Drift Mega Garchomp, and thus checks to these Pokemon such as Fur Coat Arceus-Ghost are good teammates. Powerful special wallbreakers like Mega Alakazam and Mega Sceptile appreciate the slow pivoting and entry hazards Dondozo provides and in return can offensively Imposter-proof Dondozo. Dondozo can also be Imposter-proofed by Take Heart users and Electric-types.

Flutter Mane.overview: Flutter Mane is an effective fast attacker. A Ghost typing and good Speed let it effectively use Normalize + Entrainment as a disruption tool, while more offensive sets can benefit from Moongeist Beam for high damage against Ice Scales users like Ho-Oh; Normalize in particular makes Flutter Mane an effective entry hazard setter and check to setup threats like Eternatus and some Take Heart Arceus formes. Flutter Mane also benefits from low Attack and HP, the latter of which makes Pain Split a solid choice on utility sets that lets it chunk Blissey for good damage, and decent Special Defense lets it trade damage—albeit with a high cost—against faster foes like Mega Sceptile. However, Flutter Mane's offensive profile is notably throttled by Mega Audino and Arceus-Steel, two common walls that Flutter Mane struggles to break through. It also suffers from weaknesses to common status moves like Glare and Topsy-Turvy that can stall its progress, and it, in many cases, is forced to make risky decisions thanks to said status move weakness. Flutter Mane's weak Defense also means it can't afford to stay in on stray hits like Flip Turn much, either.
Flutter Mane.comments:
Other Options

Flutter Mane's immunities to Dragon and Fighting mean it can use defensive abilities like Ice Scales and immunity abilities like Well-Baked Body and Levitate; however, this eats into damage output, making Magic Bounce users much more problematic. Flutter Mane can potentially use a Choice Specs set with either Beads of Ruin or Pixilate, but its damage falls short of competitors like Mega Alakazam, and it now struggles much more against RegenVest users. Flare Boost sets can act as potent sweepers with Nasty Plot and a built-in status immunity, but these suffer from high rates of residual damage.

Checks and Counters

Status: All Flutter Mane sets simply stop working when paralyzed; Normalize sets also falter to burn and poison. Common users also tend to carry moves like Ruination, like Chansey and Blissey, forcing Flutter Mane into unfavorable situations.

Steel-types: Arceus-Steel and Registeel resist Fairy and have good special bulk to take Ghost-type moves. The former threatens with Revelation Dance or Tachyon Cutter and can cut off Pain Split recovery with Good as Gold, while the latter threatens with Nuzzle or Gigaton Hammer. Dialga-O does not resist Fairy, but commonly uses Assault Vest, will always be carrying Revelation Dance or Doom Desire, both of which force Flutter Mane out and beat Normalize sets.

Revelation Dance and Judgment: These moves bypass Normalize, meaning that Flutter Mane essentially can't set up in front of them. Ice Scales Arceus formes also benefit from Flutter Mane still needing to use Entrainment to do meaningful damage.

Normal-types: Mega Audino, Blissey, and Meloetta all largely shut down Flutter Mane's direct damage and consistently carry Topsy-Turvy to offset Nasty Plot boosts. They tend to be fodder for Spikes, however, and only sparingly use Glare.

Magic Bounce: Magic Bounce users like Arceus-Poison and Clodsire blank most of Flutter Mane's status moves, including Spikes and Entrainment.

Utility.description:
Thanks to a combination of its great stat distribution and typing, this set turns Flutter Mane into an exceptional utility Pokemon capable of exerting long-term pressure. Magic Guard mitigates Life Orb recoil, poison damage, and entry hazard damage. Alternatively, Pixilate provides Flutter Mane with an extremely spammable STAB Boomburst, which, even without a boosting item, is capable of putting pressure to force special walls in, while Teravolt allows Flutter Mane to bypass Ice Scales and Magic Bounce. Pain Split synergizes phenomenally with Flutter Mane's low HP stat by allowing it to annoy common high-HP Pokemon like Eternatus, Blissey, and Chansey while also preventing Imposter users from freely recovering. Flutter Mane is an excellent Spikes setter with its solid longevity and many opportunities to switch in, and Spikes allows it to guarantee progress against more passive special walls like Mega Audino and Blissey. Astral Barrage gives Flutter Mane a way to severely threaten Arceus-Ghost and Solgaleo, and it further dissuades Imposter users. Knock Off is a great option to remove Assault Vest and Heavy-Duty Boots from checks, but it can be annoying to Imposter-proof. Topsy-Turvy provides more defensive utility, allowing Flutter Mane to handle boosting special attackers like Arceus formes easier. Heavy-Duty Boots allows Flutter Mane to ignore hazard damage, while Covert Cloak is useful against secondary effects from common moves like Mortal Spin and Nuzzle.

Utility Flutter Mane works well on balanced structures, though it can also perform on more offensive teams. Almost all offensive Pokemon appreciate Flutter Mane's support with Spikes. Additionally, physical wallbreakers benefit from Flutter Mane's low Attack reducing Strength Sap recovery from walls like Fur Coat Arceus formes and Zamazenta, while special wallbreakers enjoy Flutter Mane annoying special walls like Blissey and RegenVest Pokemon with Pain Split and Knock Off, respectively. Flutter Mane fits naturally into entry hazard-stacking and hazard-resilient compositions; the former appreciates its strong Spikes-setting capabilities and utility from Knock Off, while the latter eases the Imposter-proofing by being able to ignore Spikes from Imposter users. As a Spikes setter, Flutter Mane appreciates Steel-type teammates like Celesteela, Dialga-O, and Solgaleo that can block Mortal Spin and complement it defensively by switching into checks like Arceus-Steel. Imposter users generally dislike switching into Flutter Mane, but it still requires an Imposter-proof teammate such as Ho-Oh, Arceus-Steel, or RegenVest Celesteela depending on the moveset.

Normalize.description:
Normalize + Entrainment allows Flutter Mane to ignore most forms of direct damage, giving it free turns to set up or use its utility options. Judgment both ignores Normalize and allows Flutter Mane to attack with a STAB-boosted move without Imposter users being able to. Taunt shuts off annoyances like Glare and Parting Shot, and it additionally pairs well with all last-move options; Nasty Plot + Taunt makes it a solid sweeper with the rare ability to beat Topsy-Turvy users, Spikes is especially useful due to Entrainment beating all forms of entry hazard removal and forcing switches, and Thunder Cage + Taunt allows it to trap and remove targets that lack ways to damage it through Normalize. Pixie Plate has more consistent damage, while Spooky Plate is more threatening to foes like Arceus-Ghost and Imposter users.

Flutter Mane's best fit is on balance teams that can, depending on its set, either take advantage of its Spikes-setting ability or help pressure special walls that can beat Normalize. Ultra Necrozma makes a good candidate for its ability to threaten Arceus formes, which regularly carry Judgment, and Mega Audino with its mixed attacks.
